slices.title: Workspace tree scanner
slices.rollout: auto-mode canary with manual rollback flag
slices.nodeFallback: src/workspaces.js listDirectory() is used when the flag is off, the binary is missing, output is invalid, or the command fails. VIBELINK_RUST_WORKSPACE_TREE=auto skips a missing binary without marking a Rust failure.
slices.runtimeStats: rustWorkspaceTreeStats in src/workspaces.js tracks mode, auto/available state, command, hits, misses, fallbacks, failures, cache, budget hits, last signature, and last error.
slices.currentState: Rust CLI, Node opt-in/auto routing, root-directory routing, supported-subset parity, inherited and nested gitignore rules, Windows directory-size and millisecond timestamp parity, truncation, signature/cache behavior, nested gitignore invalidation, content-safe file-sample caching, fallback stats, isolated and real-repository canary harnesses, and an independent Windows CI canary gate exist. Two 2026-07-11 VibeLink checkout runs preserved exact tree/context parity, 3 cold Rust routes, 3 warm cache hits, and zero failures/fallbacks; Node ranged from 53.1-183.6ms, Rust cold from 642.5-820.4ms, and Rust warm from 11.5-16ms.
slices.nextAction: Run the real-repository canary on additional repositories and address or explicitly accept the observed process-per-miss cold-route penalty before considering default-on.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to default-on only after CI and limited real-repository sessions continue to preserve exact supported-subset parity, zero failures and fallbacks with an available command, failure-free missing-command fallback, warm cache reuse, nested gitignore invalidation, and acceptable first-launch/cold/warm latency. Rollback with VIBELINK_RUST_WORKSPACE_TREE=0 must remain tested and documented.

slices.title: Persistent MCP stdio sessions
slices.rollout: auto-mode canary with manual rollback flag
slices.nodeFallback: src/mcpRuntime.js falls back to the existing Node stdio probe/call path and closes the failed Rust sidecar client on readiness, probe, or call failure. VIBELINK_MCP_RUST_SIDECAR=auto skips a missing command without marking a Rust failure.
slices.runtimeStats: getMcpRustSidecarStats() exposes enabled, mode, auto/available/active/ready/failed state, command, args, starts, failures, fallbacks, last failure, last error, and client stats.
slices.currentState: Node manager, JSONL client, real Rust mcp-session-sidecar, auto readiness routing, contract/burst/timeout/crash/backpressure tests, synthetic and real-session canary harnesses, and an independent Windows CI gate exist. The synthetic canary reduced MCP server spawns from 13 to 1. A 2026-07-11 codebase-memory-mcp session discovered 8 tools and completed 3 real get_architecture calls at 32.6ms average and 45.5ms max with one sidecar start, zero failures/fallbacks/backpressure/pending, and clean single-session drain.
slices.nextAction: Run the real-session canary against at least one additional MCP server implementation and keep monitoring the Windows gate before considering default-on.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to default-on only after CI and limited real MCP sessions preserve zero readiness fallback after command availability, zero client and sidecar backpressure under normal load, material spawn reduction, cached tools/list behavior, clean pending drain, and tested fallback without task-visible failure. Rollback with VIBELINK_MCP_RUST_SIDECAR=0 must remain documented and tested.

slices.title: Event store append/replay sidecar
slices.rollout: auto readiness canary with manual rollback flag
slices.nodeFallback: src/db.js prefers the Rust sidecar when VIBELINK_EVENT_STORE_RUST_SIDECAR=1, or when VIBELINK_EVENT_STORE_RUST_SIDECAR=auto and the command exists. Missing binary in manual mode, bad health, timeout, invalid JSON, request failure, or sidecar exit falls back to the Node Worker when enabled, otherwise sync SQLite. Missing binary in auto mode skips Rust without marking failure.
slices.runtimeStats: getEventStoreRuntimeStats() in src/db.js exposes mode, rustSidecar enabled/auto/available/active/ready/failed, starts/failures/fallbacks/lastError/client stats, worker status, batch append stats, and eventStore metrics.
slices.currentState: Node Worker boundary, event-store metrics, batchers, JSONL sidecar client, shared method allowlist, JS fixture contract smoke, real Rust event-store-sidecar command, real Rust contract test, explicit opt-in runtime routing, auto readiness mode, health gate, Worker/sync fallback tests, rollback docs, robust trimmed-mean canary thresholds with raw latency evidence, local/runtime/server canary harnesses, CI canary status wiring, and passing representative release canaries exist. Rust batch append avoids repeated owner lookups and uses last_insert_rowid on the normal insert hot path.
slices.nextAction: Run limited human-driven real-session canaries with runtime stats capture before broader default exposure.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to default-on only after canary metrics remain within documented thresholds across automated and human-driven representative runtime sessions, CI/status checks cover contract/runtime/canary verification, and rollback remains tested and documented.

slices.title: Live audio low-latency pipeline
slices.nodeFallback: Existing live-call audio and ASR paths remain Node/provider-owned. The first Rust slice must fall back to the current Node level/stream path when missing or failed.
slices.runtimeStats: planned: sidecar stats should expose processed chunks, processed bytes, dropped chunks, backpressure rejections, and last error.
slices.currentState: Rust audio pipeline is not implemented. Current live-call stack uses existing Node/Kotlin/.NET paths and mock/real ASR integration points.
slices.nextAction: Define a bounded JSONL contract for deterministic PCM chunk preprocessing: level, peak, RMS, sequence accounting, ring-buffer/backpressure stats, __health, stats, and __close. Do not move ASR into Rust in the first slice.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to contract only after synthetic PCM tests define deterministic level/RMS behavior. Can move to opt-in only after a real sidecar exists and falls back to the existing Node path.

slices.title: Compression and context budget helper
slices.nodeEntry: src/compressionContract.js defines protocol constants for tests; no production router exists
slices.nodeFallback: Existing src/compactService.js and src/contextBudget.js behavior remains authoritative because no production path routes to this contract-only sidecar.
slices.runtimeStats: The Rust stats method exposes requests, responses, failures, bytesIn, bytesOut, timestamps, pending, and lastError.
slices.currentState: Protocol v1, shared JavaScript constants, an independent Node fixture, a real Rust compression-sidecar command, UTF-8-safe head/tail byte trimming, overlap-safe head/tail log sampling, structured errors, runtime stats, Rust unit tests, cross-language contract tests, and an independent Windows CI contract gate exist. There is intentionally no production routing, tokenizer claim, or semantic summarization.
slices.nextAction: Measure whether current Node compaction creates a material data-plane bottleneck. Only if evidence justifies native routing, add an optional Node client with missing/failed-sidecar fallback and runtime metrics before considering opt-in.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to opt-in only after measured need is documented, an optional production client exists behind VIBELINK_RUST_COMPRESSION=1, missing/failed/invalid/timeout sidecars preserve current Node behavior, runtime stats expose routing outcomes, and docs continue to state this is not semantic summarization or provider-token precision.
